title: viola coccinea
intro: Viola coccinea, also known as the crimson violet, is a perennial plant species belonging to the violet family (Violaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Viola coccinea include its ability to reduce pain, improve digestion, and act as an expectorant to relieve respiratory issues. Herbal preparations of Viola coccinea include infusions, teas, and topical creams, which can be made by steeping the leaves and flowers in hot water or using the plant's extracts in a carrier oil.
